32:
"And ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best of it: neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, lest ye die." The very brief verse notes the warning to offer the best lest they pollute the holy things and die. The grammatical voices note the warning. Wesley and Gill note that "ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best of it: neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, lest ye die" — if the Levites first offered the best, they bore no sin in eating the rest; but they must not pollute the holy things, lest they die; the ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best (no sin if the best be first offered); the neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel (the warning against profaning the holy); the lest ye die (the danger of death); the figure (the warning to give God his best first, that the rest be enjoyed without sin; the truth that to keep back the best, or to profane the holy things, brings death; the figure of the reverence due the holy things, the best given to God, the holy not polluted; the closing warning of the chapter, the holy things to be honored, the best offered, lest death follow; the reverence that honors God with the first and best, and dares not profane his holy things).
